danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprite characters with distinct silhouettes and hostile appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Threats that must be avoided or defeated via throwing/environmental interaction

danger_signals.visual_form: Quicksand in desert worlds, slippery surfaces in snowy areas
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-specific damage/movement impediment zones that affect traversal
Player character vulnerability state; loss of all hearts triggers life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Character falling off bottom screen edge
danger_signals.meaning: Instant damage/life loss mechanic, stage boundary danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating heart sprites appearing after defeating enemies
Collectible potion items scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe room mechanic; grants access to reflected zone for coin/mushroom collection without enemy pressure
Increases maximum health capacity; provides buffer against damage
Safe passage between rooms; some jars enable warping to later worlds (risk mitigation)

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rescue objective: retrieve two youngest children from Mamu's custody; family identity embedded in character roster (Mama, Papa, Imajin, Lina)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mamu appears as end-stage boss; distinct from minion enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Storybook land of Muu corrupted by Mamu's curse; final confrontation in World 7
Implied through world theming; freed/restored upon Wart's defeat in Super Mario Bros. 2 conversion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Storybook world themed with thematic hazards (desert quicksand, snowy slippery surfaces); enemies patrol environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively hostile; environmental traps persistent; no safe zones except Sub-space; player must navigate while under threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Reflected, mirrored visual representation of normal level space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies inactive/absent; player can freely collect coins and mushrooms; warping jars activate as alternate progression routes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct boss sprite (Mamu as frog-form antagonist) appears at world conclusion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss follows combat pattern; defeated via thrown objects/environmental interaction; world progression blocked until boss defeated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Implied game-over screen after final life loss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player loses all three lives; continue option available (up to two continues per game)

environmental_storytelling.example: Desert worlds contain quicksand hazards; snowy areas feature slippery surfaces. Hazard type communicates world identity and mechanical challenge without explicit narrative text.
Rooms connected via doors and ladders; jars serve dual purpose (navigation gates and Sub-space portals). Architecture suggests interconnected storybook world with hidden passages.
Different enemy types populate each world, visually distinct sprites communicate stage-specific threats and reinforce environmental theme
Heart-based health meter communicates character fragility in cursed world; floating heart rewards after combat suggest world gradually becoming less hostile

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Character moves left/right, jumps, climbs ladders/vines; can pick up and throw objects (enemies, vegetables, blocks) at other enemies
Enhanced vertical jump height but slower horizontal speed; visual cue for character specialization
Rapid object pickup and throwing; lowest jump height; specialization for fast-paced item manipulation
Short-duration float/hover mechanic; lowest speed and strength; unique traversal advantage over other characters
Patrol and attack patterns; defeated by being thrown at by player or struck with thrown objects; standing on enemies is ineffective
Distinct boss sprite; follows unique attack pattern; only defeated via environmental or thrown-object mechanics; signals world completion upon defeat
Clears all visible enemies from screen instantly; limited-use panic mechanic for danger mitigation
Temporarily freezes enemy movement; provides brief window for safe passage or attack opportunity

notes: Doki Doki Panic (Yume Koujou: Doki Doki Panic) was an advergame for Fuji Television's 1987 media expo, later adapted into Super Mario Bros. 2 for Western markets. The game communicates world-state primarily through environmental hazards, enemy placement, and character ability differentiation rather than explicit visual state systems. The storybook aesthetic (Muu kingdom, dream-logic level design) and health-based vulnerability system differentiate it from jump-on-enemy platformers. Sub-space mechanic serves as a core safety/risk management system. Faction communication is minimal; the game emphasizes personal/family narrative (Arabian family vs. evil Mamu) over complex political systems.
